paper_title: Determinants of income diversification and its effects on rural household income in Vietnam

entities.description: The process of rural households engaging in multiple income-generating activities to manage risk and improve livelihoods.
The total income earned by households living in rural areas, often analyzed for growth and stability.
The state of poverty in rural regions, which income diversification aims to alleviate.
A regression method used to analyze the number of income sources (count data).
A regression model used for censored data, applied to the Simpson index and non-farm income share.
Two-stage least squares method used to address endogeneity in the impact analysis of diversification on income.
Vietnam Household Living Standard Surveys, a digital dataset used for cross-sectional and trend analysis.
The national economic growth rate in Vietnam since the early 1990s.
The percentage of the population living in poverty in Vietnam in 2010.

entities.id: Average number of income sources in 2010 (4.36)
entities.description: The average value of the NIS measure for rural households in 2010.

entities.id: Income increase per additional source (32,977,000 VND)
entities.description: The average impact of adding one more income source on total household income.
The primary field of research concerning economic development and rural household welfare.
Researcher affiliated with University of Economics Ho Chi Minh City.
Researcher affiliated with Vietnamese - German University.
